evals.expected_output: Claude identifies slow Python patterns (manual loops, O(n) list membership, manual accumulation), rewrites them using efficient built-ins (sum(), list comprehension, set, Counter), verifies all tests still pass, benchmarks both versions, and reports a speedup.

assertions.text: Identified at least one slow pattern (O(n) list membership, manual loop accumulation, inner dict scan)
Rewrote at least one function using a more efficient Python built-in (list comprehension, sum(), dict, set, Counter)
Ran pytest to verify correctness before and after each change
Ran scripts/benchmark.py or timeit-based measurement to produce timing numbers
Reported a speedup ratio for at least one function
Used scripts/complexity.py or equivalent to measure complexity

evals.expected_output: Claude detects ruff violations, fixes them using ruff --fix then manual edits, runs ty, and reports before/after violation counts.

assertions.text: Ran ruff --fix as the first quality step before any manual edits
Fixed the bare except clause (E722)
Fixed the mutable default argument in ReportBuilder.__init__ (B006)
Ran pytest to verify tests still pass after quality fixes
Reported a before/after count of ruff violations

evals.expected_output: Claude identifies the deeply nested if-else chain in classify_order() (cc~20, nesting~5), refactors using early returns or lookup tables, reduces complexity score measurably, all tests pass.

assertions.text: Ran scripts/complexity.py --per-function to find the highest-complexity function before editing
Identified classify_order() as having high cyclomatic complexity or deep nesting
Reduced nesting depth or cyclomatic complexity (guard clauses, lookup table, or extracted helpers)
Ran pytest to verify all tests pass after simplification
Reported before/after complexity scores

evals.expected_output: Claude detects missing type annotations flagged by ty, adds proper type hints to all function signatures, verifies tests still pass, reports before/after ty error count.

assertions.text: Ran scripts/check_quality.py or ty check to identify type errors before editing
Added type annotations to at least 3 function signatures
Used appropriate types (str, list, dict, None return types, union types)
Ran pytest to verify tests still pass after adding annotations
Reported before/after ty error count

evals.expected_output: Claude identifies cross-platform issues: string path concatenation (should use pathlib.Path), missing encoding= in open() calls, os.path.* usage (ruff PTH rules), and hardcoded path separators. Fixes them using pathlib.Path and explicit encoding='utf-8', verifies tests pass on any OS.

assertions.text: Identified cross-platform issues: string path concatenation, missing encoding=, or os.path.* usage
Replaced string path concatenation (dirpath + '/' + filename) with pathlib.Path or os.path.join
Added encoding='utf-8' to open() calls that were missing it
Ran pytest to verify tests still pass after cross-platform fixes
Reported ruff violation count before and after (PTH rules and SIM rules)

evals.expected_output: Claude identifies shell=True used with list commands (redundant and platform-fragile), missing encoding= in open() calls, os.path.* usage, and makedirs without exist_ok. Fixes them for cross-platform safety, verifies tests pass.

assertions.text: Identified shell=True used with list-form commands (redundant, breaks on Windows)
Removed shell=True from subprocess.run() calls that pass a list (not a string)
Added encoding='utf-8' to open() calls missing it
Fixed os.makedirs without exist_ok=True to avoid race condition
Ran pytest to verify tests still pass after fixes

evals.expected_output: Claude identifies and fixes all three goal types: ruff violations (import style, comparison to None, type() instead of isinstance()), slow algorithms (bubble sort, manual O(n) dict grouping), and high complexity (nested loops). Runs pytest throughout. Reports all three metric deltas.

assertions.text: Replaced the manual bubble sort in top_n_per_category with sorted()
Fixed ruff violations (import style, type() == instead of isinstance(), mutable default None comparison)
Reduced nesting in filter_records using early continue or guard clauses
Ran pytest after each change to verify correctness
Reported all three metric types: speed (ms), quality (ruff+ty count), complexity score

evals.expected_output: Claude identifies O(n) list-of-tuples store (should be a dict for O(1) lookup), missing type annotations on all methods, and type() == comparisons. Replaces the store with a dict or collections.OrderedDict, adds type hints, fixes ruff violations. All 14 tests pass.

assertions.text: Identified that the list-of-tuples store causes O(n) get/set/has/delete operations
Replaced list-of-tuples store with a dict (or OrderedDict) for O(1) lookup
Added type annotations to at least 3 method signatures in SimpleCache
Fixed type() == comparisons to use isinstance() in update_many()
Ran pytest to verify all cache tests pass after the restructure
